nominee.definition: A person named, or designated, by another, to any office, duty, or position; one nominated, or proposed, by others for office or for election to office.

nonsense.definition: Letters or words, in writing or speech, that have no meaning or pattern or seem to have no meaning.

noon.definition: The ninth hour of the day counted from sunrise; around three o'clock in the afternoon.

notable.definition: A person or thing of distinction.

notably.definition: (focus) As a pointed example; in a notable manner.

notorious.definition: Widely known, especially for something negative; infamous.

novel.definition: Newly made, formed or evolved; having no precedent; of recent origin; new.

nursery.definition: A place where nursing or the raising of children is carried on.

O.definition: The name of the Latin-script letter O.

oblige.definition: To constrain someone by force or by social, moral or legal means.

obsess.definition: (passive, constructed with "with") To be preoccupied with a single topic or emotion.

occasional.definition: A person who does something only occasionally.

occurrence.definition: An actual instance when a situation occurs; an event or happening.

offering.definition: To propose or express one's willingness (to do something).

offspring.definition: A person's daughter(s) and/or son(s); a person's children.

operational.definition: Of or relating to operations, especially military operations.

optical.definition: Of, or relating to sight; visual.

optimism.definition: A tendency to expect the best, or at least, a favourable outcome

oral.definition: A spoken test or examination, particularly in a language class.

organisational.definition: Of, relating to, or produced by an organization.

orientation.definition: The determination of the relative position of something or someone.

originate.definition: To cause to be, to bring into existence; to produce, initiate.

outbreak.definition: An eruption; the sudden appearance of a rash, disease, etc.

outlet.definition: A vent or similar passage to allow the escape of something.

outlook.definition: A place from which something can be viewed.

outrage.definition: An excessively violent or vicious attack; an atrocity.

outsider.definition: One who is not part of a community or organization.

overlook.definition: A vista or point that gives a beautiful view.

overly.definition: Superficial; not thorough; careless, negligent, inattentive.

oversee.definition: To survey, look at something in a wide angle.

overturn.definition: A turning over or upside-down; inversion.

overwhelm.definition: The state or condition of being overwhelmed.

overwhelming.definition: To engulf, surge over and submerge.

pad.definition: A flattened mass of anything soft, to sit or lie on.

parameter.definition: A value kept constant during an experiment, equation, calculation or similar, but varied over other versions of the experiment, equation, calculation, etc.

parental.definition: A person fulfilling a parental role.

parliament.definition: A formal council summoned (especially by a monarch) to discuss important issues.

partial.definition: A partial derivative: a derivative with respect to one independent variable of a function in multiple variables while holding the other variables constant.

partially.definition: To a partial degree or extent, incompletely.

pastor.definition: A shepherd; someone who tends to a flock of animals.

patent.definition: A declaration issued by a government agency declaring someone the inventor of a new invention and having the privilege of stopping others from making, using or selling the claimed invention; a letter patent.

pathway.definition: A footpath or other path or track.

patrol.definition: A going of the rounds along the chain of sentinels and between the posts, by a guard, usually consisting of three or four men, to insure greater security from attacks on the outposts.

patron.definition: One who protects or supports; a defender or advocate.

peak.definition: A point; the sharp end or top of anything that terminates in a point; as, the peak, or front, of a cap.

peasant.definition: A member of the lowly social class that toils on the land, constituted by small farmers and tenants, sharecroppers, farmhands and other laborers on the land where they form the main labor force in agriculture and horticulture.

peculiar.definition: That which is peculiar; a sole or exclusive property; a prerogative; a characteristic.

pension.definition: An annuity paid regularly as benefit due to a retired employee, serviceman etc. in consideration of past services, originally and chiefly by a government but also by various private pension schemes.

persist.definition: To go on stubbornly or resolutely.

persistent.definition: Obstinately refusing to give up or let go.

petition.definition: A formal, written request made to an official person or organized body, often containing many signatures.

pioneer.definition: One who goes before, as into the wilderness, preparing the way for others to follow.

pipeline.definition: A conduit made of pipes used to convey water, gas or petroleum etc.

pirate.definition: A criminal who plunders at sea; commonly attacking merchant vessels, though often pillaging port towns.

plea.definition: An appeal, petition, urgent prayer or entreaty.

plead.definition: To present (an argument or a plea), especially in a legal case.

pledge.definition: A solemn promise to do something.

plug.definition: A pronged connecting device which fits into a mating socket, especially an electrical one.

plunge.definition: The act of plunging or submerging

pole.definition: Originally, a stick; now specifically, a long and slender piece of metal or (especially) wood, used for various construction or support purposes.

poll.definition: A survey of people, usually statistically analyzed to gauge wider public opinion.

pond.definition: An inland body of standing water, either natural or man-made, that is smaller than a lake.

pop.definition: A loud, sharp sound as of a cork coming out of a bottle.

portfolio.definition: A case for carrying papers, drawings, photographs, maps and other flat documents.

portray.definition: To paint or draw the likeness of.

postpone.definition: To delay or put off an event, appointment etc.

practitioner.definition: A person who practices a profession or art, especially law or medicine.

precedent.definition: An act in the past which may be used as an example to help decide the outcome of similar instances in the future.

precision.definition: The state of being precise or exact; exactness.

predator.definition: Any animal or other organism that hunts and kills other organisms (their prey), primarily for food.

predecessor.definition: One who precedes; one who has preceded another in any state, position, office, etc.; one whom another follows or comes after, in any office or position.

predominantly.definition: In a predominant manner. Most commonly or frequently by a large margin.

prejudice.definition: An adverse judgment or opinion formed beforehand or without knowledge of the facts.

preliminary.definition: A preparation for a main matter; an introduction.

premier.definition: (Westminster system) The head of government in parliament and leader of the cabinet.

premise.definition: A proposition antecedently supposed or proved; something previously stated or assumed as the basis of further argument; a condition; a supposition.

prescribe.definition: To order (a drug or medical device) for use by a particular patient (under licensed authority).

prescription.definition: The act of prescribing a rule, law, etc..

preservation.definition: The act of preserving; care to preserve; act of keeping from destruction, decay or any ill.

preside.definition: To act as president or chairperson.

presidency.definition: The office or role of president.
